title: Arrest Rate by Crime Type
finding_preview: Arrest rates vary significantly by crime type — NARCOTICS highest (~45%), MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT lowest (~10%).
finding_full: Arrest rates vary significantly by crime type. NARCOTICS has the highest arrest rate (~45%), while MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT has the lowest (~10%). This suggests that arrest likelihood is strongly influenced by the nature of the offense.

title: Arrest Rate by Year
finding_preview: Overall arrest rate is stable at approximately 21-22% across 2024-2026.
finding_full: The overall arrest rate is stable across years at approximately 21-22%. No significant year-over-year change in arrest rates is observed in the synthetic data.

title: Arrest Rate by District
finding_preview: Arrest rates vary by district — District 8 highest (~25%), District 23 lowest (~19%).
finding_full: Arrest rates vary by district — District 8 has the highest arrest rate (~25%) while District 23 has the lowest (~19%). Downtown-oriented districts tend to have higher arrest rates than outlying areas.

title: Arrest Rate by Location Type
finding_preview: BAR OR TAVERN crimes have the highest arrest rate (~24%), while GAS STATION crimes are lowest (~17%).
finding_full: Arrest rates vary by location type. BAR OR TAVERN crimes have the highest arrest rate (~24%), followed by APARTMENT (~23%) and RESIDENCE (~22%). GAS STATION and PARKING LOT locations have lower rates (~17-18%).

title: Domestic vs Non-Domestic Crimes
finding_preview: Domestic crimes: 15% of total, 25% arrest rate. BATTERY dominates domestic incidents.
finding_full: Domestic crimes account for ~15% of all crimes. They have a higher arrest rate (~25%) compared to non-domestic crimes (~21%). BATTERY is the dominant domestic crime type, accounting for ~40% of domestic incidents.

title: Hotspot Stability Over Time
finding_preview: District rankings are highly stable across years. Top 3 districts remain consistent.
finding_full: District rankings are highly stable across years. The top 3 districts remain consistent from 2024 to 2026, suggesting persistent geographic concentration of crime. This stability implies that resource allocation decisions based on historical data are likely to remain relevant.

title: Month-by-Location Heatmap
finding_preview: STREET locations show the strongest seasonal variation. RESIDENCE locations are more stable.
finding_full: STREET locations show the strongest seasonal variation, with summer peaks. RESIDENCE locations are more stable across months. This suggests that outdoor crime is more seasonal than indoor crime.

title: District-by-Month Heatmap
finding_preview: All districts show similar seasonal patterns with summer peaks.
finding_full: All districts show similar seasonal patterns with summer peaks. The top-volume districts (6, 7, 8) show the strongest seasonal amplitude, while low-volume districts are flatter.

title: Location-by-Day Heatmap
finding_preview: STREET crimes peak on Friday and Saturday nights. RESIDENCE crimes are more even.
finding_full: STREET crimes peak on Friday and Saturday nights. RESIDENCE crimes are more evenly distributed across days. This confirms that weekend effects are driven primarily by outdoor/public-space crimes.

title: Crime Co-occurrence by Area
finding_preview: THEFT and BATTERY co-occur in high-volume districts. NARCOTICS clusters independently.
finding_full: Crime types show moderate co-occurrence patterns. THEFT and BATTERY tend to co-occur in high-volume districts. NARCOTICS shows weaker co-occurrence with other types, suggesting it clusters independently.

title: Neighborhood Clustering by Crime Profile
finding_preview: Districts cluster into 4 distinct crime archetypes based on type mix and arrest rates.
finding_full: Districts cluster into 4 distinct crime archetypes based on type mix and arrest rates. Cluster 1 (high-volume, low-arrest) represents south-side districts; Cluster 2 (balanced) represents north-side districts.

title: Crime Diversity Across Community Areas
finding_preview: Downtown areas show higher crime diversity. Outlying areas are dominated by fewer types.
finding_full: Crime diversity (Shannon entropy) varies across community areas. Some areas have a balanced mix of crime types (high diversity), while others are dominated by 1-2 types (low diversity). High-diversity areas tend to be in the city center.

title: Correlation Between Crime Type and Time
finding_preview: THEFT correlates with daytime; BATTERY and ASSAULT correlate with nighttime.
finding_full: THEFT correlates with daytime hours (10am-6pm), while BATTERY and ASSAULT correlate with nighttime hours (8pm-2am). NARCOTICS shows no strong temporal correlation, occurring uniformly across hours.

title: Correlation Between Crime Type and Location
finding_preview: THEFT associates with STREET/PARKING LOT. BATTERY associates with RESIDENCE/APARTMENT.
finding_full: THEFT is strongly associated with STREET and PARKING LOT locations. BATTERY is associated with RESIDENCE and APARTMENT locations. This confirms that theft tends to occur in public spaces while battery tends to occur in private spaces.
